title: Executive Employment Agreement with Single-Trigger Change of Control

issues.rationale: Single-trigger change of control (full acceleration upon change of control without requiring termination) creates a perverse incentive for the executive to facilitate a sale regardless of shareholder interest. Double-trigger (acceleration only if terminated after CIC) is the market standard. This is an unusual provision that weakens corporate governance.

ground_truth.rationale: The single-trigger change of control is the primary issue. It accelerates all unvested equity upon change of control alone, without requiring termination. This creates a misalignment between executive and shareholder interests. The 24-month severance (if terminated after CIC) is generous but secondary. Other terms (12-month non-compete, reasonable invention assignment) are standard.
